calculated_metrics.confidence_note: This unit cost is estimated by PRISM by dividing total programme expenditure by the total quantified animal beneficiaries.

details.calculation: ($614,113 USD / 69,193 total beneficiaries) = $8.88 USD per outcome. | A $1,000 USD donation achieves ≈ 113 outcomes.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

details.formula: Mapping of verified intervention types to the Intervention Leverage Tier framework.

details.elaboration: Quote: 'We worked with two cage-free producers to get them certified and eight hotels to help them replace 1.3 million eggs from caged sources with cage-free sources.'
details.formula: Highest level of self-reported evidence cited in impact metrics

details.calculation: No quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

details.calculation: Mixed portfolio with minority focus on high-neglectedness areas (Wild Animals: 39%, Companion Animals: 44%, Unspecified: 17%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: No disclosure of unintended negative impacts found. This is the industry norm.
details.elaboration: This check rewards epistemic humility. Non-disclosure is standard and not a failure.
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: Organisation does not engage in direct animal sheltering; metric not applicable.
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

source.quote: Expenditure towards programs...5,07,53,120₹
Income from grants and voluntary contributions.........13,53,88,680₹
A total of 30, 215 street dogs were sterilized and vaccinated this year across 3 projects

Our work on olive ridley turtle conservation through our partner organization saw 26,978 olive ridley hatchlings being released back to the ocean after their vulnerable nests were relocated to hatcheries and incubated.
Provided relief to over 12,000 animals in Kerala, Odisha and Assam during floods
Our mission is to protect ALL animals: farmed animals, animals in laboratories, companion animals, wildlife and those found in disaster or cruelty situations.

metrics.context_qualifier: 1.3 million eggs from caged sources replaced with cage-free sources by eight hotels.

source.quote: We worked with two cage-free producers to get them certified and eight hotels to help them replace 1.3 million eggs from caged sources with cage-free sources.

metrics.context_qualifier: Street dogs and cats sterilized and vaccinated across 3 projects in India.

source.quote: A total of 30, 215 street dogs were sterilized and vaccinated this year across 3 projects

metrics.context_qualifier: Olive Ridley Turtle hatchlings released back to the ocean after nest relocation and incubation.

source.quote: Our work on olive ridley turtle conservation through our partner organization saw 26,978 olive ridley hatchlings being released back to the ocean after their vulnerable nests were relocated to hatcheries and incubated.

metrics.context_qualifier: Animals provided relief during floods in Kerala, Odisha, and Assam.

source.quote: Provided relief to over 12,000 animals in Kerala, Odisha and Assam during floods

metrics.context_qualifier: Animal-based meals replaced with plant-based alternatives in businesses.

source.quote: We trained over 350 culinary students on plant-based cooking and received commitments from several homemakers and restaurants to replace over 100,000 animal-based meals in their businesses with plant-based ones.

metrics.context_qualifier: People reached through awareness campaigns on human-snake conflict mitigation.

source.quote: Reached out to over 200,000 people creating awareness on human-snake conflict mitgation through QR codes, fairs and IEC materials

metrics.context_qualifier: Battery-cage poultry facilities issued closure notices by Haryana State Pollution Control Board for pollution.

source.quote: 18 battery-cage facilities were sent notices to for pollution by the Haryana State Pollution Board

metrics.context_qualifier: Government officers trained on animal law and responding to cruelty situations across Karnataka and West Bengal.

source.quote: Over 1000 government officers were trained including police officers, veterinary doctors and forest department officials across Karnataka and West Bengal

metrics.context_qualifier: Meetings conducted to address human-dog conflicts, waste disposal, and feeding stations.

source.quote: Over the past year, we conducted 842 meetings that reached more than 5,212 community members, focusing on topics such as managing human-dog conflicts, proper waste disposal and the establishment of feeding and ...

metrics.context_qualifier: Individuals supported in pursuing legal action for animal cruelty cases.

source.quote: Our efforts to address animal cruelty continued, with support provided to over 12 individuals pursuing legal action for animal cruelty cases.

metrics.context_qualifier: Cumulative number of dogs sterilized and vaccinated through their program since 2013.

source.quote: 400,000+ dogs have been sterilized and vaccinated through our program since 2013.

metrics.context_qualifier: Cumulative number of animals saved by India-based disaster response team since 2018.

source.quote: Our India-based disaster response team has saved more than 250,000 animals' lives since 2018.

significant_events.event_name: Indian Government Passes Law for Non-Animal Methodologies in Drug Discovery

source.quote: Government of India brought in a law allowing non animal methodologies to be used in pre-clinical trials for drugs, a first ever in India

significant_events.summary: Indian government passed a law allowing non-animal methodologies in pre-clinical trials for drugs, a first in India.

significant_events.event_name: 8 State Governments Reiterate Ban on Gestation and Farrowing Crates

source.quote: Animal husbandry departments of 8 STATE GOVERNMENT issues orders reiterating the PROHIBITION ON GESTATION AND FARROWING CRATES, potentially IMPACTING OVER 950,000 PIGS across these states.

significant_events.summary: Animal husbandry departments of 8 state governments issued orders reiterating the prohibition on gestation and farrowing crates, potentially impacting over 950,000 pigs.

source.quote: We helped ban animal testing of cosmetics in India and negotiated rules for pesticides and vaccines that have spared thousands of animals from severe suffering and death in needless testing.

significant_events.summary: Helped ban animal testing of cosmetics in India and negotiated rules for pesticides and vaccines, sparing thousands of animals.

significant_events.event_name: Model District Program in West Bengal

source.quote: OUR MODEL DISTRICT PROGRAM in West Bengal saw HSI/India being inducted as member of the State Animal Welfare Board and our staff as members in the Society for Prevention of Cruelty to Animals across districts.

significant_events.summary: HSI/India inducted as member of State Animal Welfare Board and staff as members in Society for Prevention of Cruelty to Animals across districts.

significant_events.event_name: India's First Operation Manual on Human-Snake Conflict Management

source.quote: Government of Karnataka released India's FIRST OPERATION MANUAL ON HUMAN - SNAKE CONFLICT MANAGEMENT for Karnataka, which was CO-AUTHORED BY HSI INDIA.

significant_events.summary: Government of Karnataka released India's first operation manual on human-snake conflict management, co-authored by HSI India.

source.quote: We launched a snakebite app in collaboration with The Liana Trust to provide easily accessible information about snake species found in the local vicinity, as well as lifesaving snakebite first-aid, snakebite prevention tips, and myth-busting around misinformation about snakes that can lead to acts of cruelty.

significant_events.summary: Launched a snakebite app in collaboration with The Liana Trust to provide information on snake species, first-aid, prevention, and myth-busting.

source.quote: Lucknow has achieved a major milestone in humane street dog management by completing one 100,000 dog sterilization surgeries under its Animal Birth Control program, jointly implemented.

significant_events.summary: Lucknow completed 100,000 dog sterilization surgeries under its Animal Birth Control program.

source.quote: Rudrapur celebrates successful humane street dog population management, with 95% dog sterilization rate.

significant_events.summary: Rudrapur celebrates successful humane street dog population management with a 95% dog sterilization rate.

significant_events.event_name: First Swarnali Roy Vegan Advocacy Awards

source.quote: THE FIRST SWARNALI ROY VEGAN ADVOCACY AWARDS were announced, and three vegan champions were awarded prizes amounting to 1,00,000 INR.

significant_events.summary: Three vegan champions were awarded prizes amounting to 1,00,000 INR.

significant_events.event_name: Disaster Response to Landslides and Floods in Wayanad

source.quote: Humane World India deployed its animal rescue team to aid relief efforts after devastating landslides and floods hit Wayanad in the summer of 2024.

significant_events.summary: Deployed animal rescue team to aid relief efforts after landslides and floods in Wayanad, providing shelter, feed, medical supplies.

significant_events.event_name: Kerala State Disaster Management Authority Commissioned Study

source.quote: We were commissioned by the Kerala State Disaster Management Authority to study animal vulnerability, a foundational step for data-driven disaster planning.

significant_events.summary: Commissioned by Kerala State Disaster Management Authority to study animal vulnerability, a foundational step for data-driven disaster planning.

source.quote: 86% sterilization and vaccination rate achieved in Vadodara
Unfortunately, this year we also suffered another loss of an employee. Lallu, our team member in Lucknow, lost his life to a chronic illness, leaving behind his family and leaving our staff in shock.

data.original_text: Humane World for Animals is one of the few groups in India striving to protect all animals.
We want to give farmed animals freedom to move, and we advocate for a more plant-based food system.
We want to transition testing and research to animal-free methods and manage street dog populations with compassion.
We aid animals in crisis situations, and we teach people how to peacefully coexist with animals, be it on the streets with dogs or wild animals in nature.
Our India-based disaster response team has saved more than 250,000 animals' lives since 2018.
We assist with preparedness efforts to help communities safely evacuate with their animals before a disaster.
When one strikes, we deploy quickly to provide food, water and veterinary treatment.
We're working to end animal testing for good.
400,000+ dogs have been sterilized and vaccinated through our program since 2013.
250,000+ animals have been saved through our disaster response team's efforts.
0 animals are used in cosmetics testing in India, thanks to years of our advocacy.
Humane World India deployed its animal rescue team to aid relief efforts after devastating landslides and floods hit Wayanad in the summer of 2024.
We provided shelter for affected animals and 200kgs of emergency animal feed to stranded cattle, along with as medical and veterinary supplies, food and water.

We launched a snakebite app in collaboration with The Liana Trust to provide easily accessible information about snake species found in the local vicinity, as well as lifesaving snakebite first-aid, snakebite prevention tips, and myth-busting around misinformation about snakes that can lead to acts of cruelty.
India has close to 70 million street dogs.
We help humanely manage this population by working with veterinarians, local governments and communities on spay/neuter and promoting peaceful coexistence with street dogs.
We helped ban animal testing of cosmetics in India and negotiated rules for pesticides and vaccines that have spared thousands of animals from severe suffering and death in needless testing.
We're working with scientists, businesses and the government to accelerate the development and use of animal-free approaches for testing and research.
Lucknow has achieved a major milestone in humane street dog management by completing one 100,000 dog sterilization surgeries under its Animal Birth Control program, jointly implemented.
Rudrapur celebrates successful humane street dog population management, with 95% dog sterilization rate.

Humane World for Animals India (formerly called Humane Society International/India) is a nonprofit with a proven track record of addressing the root causes of animal cruelty and suffering to create permanent change.

We are doing this through mitigating human-animal conflicts, building disaster response and resilience, capacity-building of enforcement officials, safeguarding street animals, protecting farmed animals, and influencing public policy to reduce animal suffering used for experimentation.
We protect ALL animals!
Beneficiaries: Children and youths. Environment. People with medical needs. Women and girls.
Through spay and neuter and educational campaigns, our programs are changing hearts and minds while providing essential veterinary care and resources the dogs need.
In the capital city of Dehradun, over 40,000 dogs have been sterilized since 2017, including over 6,000 animals brought in by community members.
We're also fighting to end the cruel dog meat trade in India.
We're teaching rural communities how to avoid conflict with elephants and snakes.

In India, we operate in 10+ States and work across several critical areas such as cruelty to animals, street dog management, relief and rehabilitation for animals during disasters, mitigating human- wildlife conflict, building capacity of law enforcement officers on animal welfare laws and its enforcement, farm-animal Welfare and more.
We replaced 134,000 new meals in 2024-2025.
We worked with two cage-free producers to get them certified and eight hotels to help them replace 1.3 million eggs from caged sources with cage-free sources.
The team addressed more than 9,210 complaints and requests related to street dog welfare, underscoring its responsiveness and community-driven approach.
Additionally, 751 street dogs and three cats were vaccinated with community sponsorship in Dehradun and Lucknow.

Over the past year, we conducted 842 meetings that reached more than 5,212 community members, focusing on topics such as managing human-dog conflicts, proper waste disposal and the establishment of feeding and ...
We reached 600,000 people via community radio in Wayanad on May 2024, with our message translated into tribal languages, increasing disaster preparedness awareness for animals.
We were commissioned by the Kerala State Disaster Management Authority to study animal vulnerability, a foundational step for data-driven disaster planning.
successfully sterilized and vaccinated 24,268 street dogs and 33 cats across our program locations.
Our program achieved significant sterilization coverage in key locations Lucknow (84.3%), Dehradun (85%), Mussoorie (92%) and Nainital (99%).
With the successful completion of our goals in Nainital, the program there has now been formally concluded.
The dog population remained stable, evidenced by a low proportion of lactating females (2.1%) and puppies (1.8%).
Baseline surveys in Rudrapur (2024) and Kashipur (2025) showed zero sterilization among dogs before the launch of the Animal Birth Control (ABC) programme in those areas.
Additionally, we collaborated with the Dehradun District Panchayat to conduct a joint manual dog count in selected rural villages, providing a comprehensive report as requested.
Our efforts to address animal cruelty continued, with support provided to over 12 individuals pursuing legal action for animal cruelty cases.

aliases: Humane World for Animals India Foundation
